original: Test Cricket debut of Lawrence Rowe WI v NZ Kingston, 214 & 100
Howard Carteropens the inner burial chamber of Egyptian Pharaoh Tutankhamun's tomb and finds the sarcophagus
Detroit Red Wings' captain Alex Delvecchio picks up an assist in a 6-2 defeat at Minnesota to become the 3rd player in NHL history to score 1,000 career points
Italy completes the bobsleigh double at the Grenoble Winter Olympics with victory in the 4-man; Eugenio Monti and Luciano de Paolis double up for gold winning 2-man earlier
Bill Johnson becomes first American to win an Olympic downhill skiing gold at the Sarajevo Games; beats Swiss Peter Müller by 0.27s
Johann Olav Koss skates world record 1500m (1:51.29)
China announces it will relocate 9,000 people in Guizhou province, before completion of world's largest telescope (FAST), designed to look for extraterrestrial life
'Ghost ship' cargo vessel MV Alta washed up on the Irish coast near Ballycotton by Storm Dennis, after drifting across the Atlantic from Bermuda
Lionel Messiscores his 14th consecutive goal in La Liga and his 300th goal in 365 appearances for Barcelona
Abraham Lincolnstops his train at Westfield on his way to Washington to thank 11-year-old Grace Bedell in person for her advice to grow a beard to gain more votes
Bradford Parkinson, American engineer and inventor (Global Positioning System), born in Madison, Wisconsin
Australian cricket batsman Bob Cowper makes a patient 307 (727 mins, 20 x 4s) in drawn 5th Test v England in Melbourne
Sandra Völker swims world record 50m backstroke (28.33 sec)
Needing 78 for an unlikely victory, Sri Lanka's Kusal Perera 153no and Vishwa Fernando 6no put on a record 10th wicket stand for a 1 wicket win vs South Africa in the 1st cricket Test in Durban
Johannes Høsflot Klæbo teams with Erik Valnes to win the men's team sprint for Norway and clinch the cross country freestyle sprint double at the Beijing Winter Olympics
Second appearance of the Beatles on "TheEd SullivanShow", live from Deauville Hotel in Miami, Florida; broadcast draws about 70 million television viewers
Anna Mae Hays [McCabe], American military officer and US military's 1st female General, born in Buffalo, New York (d. 2018)
Pegasus 1 launched to detect micro-meteors
-59°F (-51°C), Pokegama Dam, Minnesota (state record)
Assembled STS-3 vehicle moves from Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) to launch pad
More than 100,00 orangutans killed in Borneo since 1999 according to study published in "Current Biology"
Swiss cross country skier Dario Cologna wins 4th career Olympic gold medal in the 15k freestyle in Pyeongchang; wins event in Vancouver (2010), and 15k classical & 30k skiathlon in Sochi (2014)
NASA's Explorer 9 is the first spacecraft placed in orbit by an all-solid rocket and the first spacecraft successfully launched into orbit from Wallops Island in Virginia
China uses its 1st nuclear reactor
US nuclear submarine USS Triton set off on underwater round-world trip
The Beatles top British rock charts with "Please, Please Me"
French astronomerCharles Messierpresents his original list of 45 M-objects to French Academy
Lithuania declares independence from Russia & Germany (National Day)
The "Toddlers' Truce", a controversial television close down between 6 and 7 pm, abolished in the United Kingdom
Swedish cross country skier Gunde Svan takes his 2nd gold medal of the Sarajevo Winter Olympics in the 4 x 10k relay; also wins 15k gold
Hugo de Vries, Dutch botanist and geneticist who rediscovered Mendel's theory of heredity and for his work on genes (The Mutation Theory), born in Haarlem, Netherlands (d. 1935)
Vera Menchik, Russian-born British chess master, 1st official women's world chess champion (1927-39), born in Moscow, Russian Empire (d. 1944)
LeVar Burton, American actor, (Roots - "Kunta Kinte"; Star Trek: The Next Generation - "Lt. Cmdr. Geordi La Forge"), born in Landstuhl, Germany
US Navy Lt Stephen Decatur raids Tripoli Harbor & burns Navy frigate "Philadelphia" after it is seized by pirates
AmericanCharles Wilkesdiscovers the Shackleton Ice Shelf in Antarctica
9th recorded perihelion passage of Halley's Comet
Pope Gregory the Great decrees saying "God bless You" is the correct response to a sneeze
Gallaudet College (National Deaf Mute college) forms in Washington, D.C.
-32°F (-36°C), Falls Village, Connecticut (state record)
NYC passes bill prohibiting racism in city-assisted housing
Swedish cross country skier Thomas Wassberg wins 2nd career gold medal in 4 x 10k relay at Sarajevo Winter Olympics; 2nd of 4 career gold; 15k Lake Placid (1980), 50k Sarajevo, relay Calgary (1988)
Athens and parts of Greece covered in unusual heavy snowfall
WWII: US Naval History and Heritage Command confirms identity of the wreck USS Albacore, a submarine lost in 1944, discovered by University of Tokyo sonar team in 2022 off of Hokkaidō, Japan
Suzanne Lenglendefeats US champion Helen Wills in influential tennis match in Cannes, France
Joe Frazier(26) TKOs Jimmy Ellis (29) in 5 rounds for undisputed heavyweight boxing title, at Madison Square Garden, NYC
Mary Ewing Outerbridge, American citizen credited with importing tennis to the US from Bermuda (1874), born in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania (d. 1886)
Robert R. Williams, American chemist, first to synthesize vitamins thiamine and B1, born in Nellore, India (d. 1965)[1]
James Baskett, American actor (Song of the South) 1st African American male to receive an Oscar, born in Indianapolis, Indiana (d. 1948)
Jill Kinmont Boothe, American skier paralysed in a ski accident, had two films portray her life, born in Los Angeles, California (d. 2012)
Miranda, famous moon of Uranus, photographed for 1st time
San Francisco City Hall dome fire
1st commercially designed helicopter tested, Bridgeport, Connecticut
Largest NBA crowd to date, 43,816, sees Philadelphia at Detroit
Mathieu Schneider of the Los Angeles Kings becomes the first defenseman in history to score a goal against all 30 NHL teams as LA beats Minnesota, 4-0
Kyoto Protocol comes into force following its ratification by Russia, committing industrialized nations to limit and reduce greenhouse gas emissions
America evacuates 400 citizens from COVID-19-infected cruise ship Diamond Princess quarantined in Yokohama port, Japan
US Winter storm continues with Dallas recording its lowest temperature in 70 years minus-2 degrees Fahrenheit
Saturday Night Live celebrates 50 years with a special show “SNL50: The Anniversary Special” with celebrity guests and returning cast members
Wilt Chamberlainof the Los Angeles Lakers becomes first player in NBA history to reach the career 30,000 point mark during a 110-109 loss to the Phoenix Suns
Livingston Bramble defeatsRay Mancinito win WBA lightweight championship, in Buffalo, New York
